event_info: Arshdeep shortens the length and pushes one down the leg-side as Samson walked at the bowler; past the flick and is called as a wide
the inswinger from Arshdeep - Samson leans across; covers the line and gets a thick inside edge to mid-wicket

nicely played; short of a good length and angling into the right-hander - Samson leans across; lets the ball come and then tucks it sweetly past square leg. Long chase for Shashank in the deep and he does well to prevent the boundary
on a good length and at the stumps - Ruturaj prods across and turns it onto the on-side
fraction fuller and just outside off - Gaikwad leans across into an open-faced dab; behind square on the off-side for a single
beauty; short of a good length and pushed across the right-hander; a hint of bounce and Samson is beaten on the poke; beaten by the angle
short of a good length and around off; Samson leans across and defends it to the on-side
nicely played; on a good length and wide ofd off; friendly start from Bartlett and Ruturaj pushes it into the gap wide of cover; runs away across the fast outfield
short of a good length and just outside off - Gaikwad hops across defensively
tad fuller and wider of off; eased into the gap wide of cover for a single
beaten; on a good length and shaping away from outside off; draws Samson into the drive and he's beaten on the outside edge
thumped; on a good length and well wide of off - Samson stands tall and then drives it on the rise; was in the air and in the gap wide of mid-off

ball_event: out Caught by Prabhsimran Singh!! Edged and caught! Second failure in a row and Chepauk has been silenced. On a good length and nipping away from off
event_info: Samson backed away on the drive; doens't cover for the movement and gets a thin edge through to the keeper. A simple catch and Prabhsimran Singh doesn't miss out. Sanju Samson c Prabhsimran Singh b Xavier Bartlett 7(7) [4s-1]
cracking shot; back of a length and well wide of off - Gaikwad stands tall and then punches it into the gap wide of cover-point; beats the fielder sliding across in the deep
short of a good length and at the stumps; nudged with soft hands towards mid-wicket
short of a good length and at the stumps; Mhatre prods across defensively
change of pace from Jansen - slower ball at 119 kmph; Mhatre prods half-forward and inside edges halves it back at the bowler
Jansen loses his line and spills one down leg - the swing takes it further away and Prabhsimran Singh can only palm it away; runs away to the ropes for extra wides
short of a good length and well wide of off; stabbed away from his body and Mhatre picks out cover
fuller length delivery outside off - Mhatre clears his front-leg on the drive and finds mid-off; on the bounce to the fielder
short of a length and at the stumps; tucked past square leg for a single

an agricultural heave and Mhatre gets off the mark with a boundary; pitched up by Bartlett - Mhatre gets his left leg out of the way and heaves it over mid-on; came off the inner half; but clears the fielder in the ring with ease
much better from Mhatre - too full and too straight from Bartlett; Mhatre lets the ball come; held his shape and then whipped it over mid-wicket for a boundary

a miscue; but Mhatre has three fours in a row - Bartlett dragged the length back and Mhatre wasn't in a great position on the pull; slightly muscled and he clears square leg with ease; one bounce and over the ropes
back of a length and just outside off - Mhatre swivels across and then clubs the pull wide of mid-on - Arshdeep gets across to his left at long-on; puts in a clumsy slide and makes the stop
fuller length delivery outside off; an open-faced drive; but straight to the fielder at backward point; 15 off the over
short of a good length and angling back into the right-hander; dabbed backward of square on the off-side for a single
on a good length and angling back into the right-hander; Mhatre swings across the line and misses. Comes off the pad and rolls to the off-side

slugged; back of a length and at the stumps; looked like the slower off-cutter from Vyshak. Mhatre gets his left leg out of the way and then hoists it high over wide long-on; sailed over the ropes for a maximum
on a good length and at the stumps; Mhatre swings through the line and places it wide of mid-wicket; the fielder in the deep gets across and keeps them down to a couple
on a good length and just outside off - Mhatre stays leg-sided and then slogs across the line; misses and the ball bounces through to the keeper
short of a good length and at the stumps - Mhatre swivels across and pulls it past square leg for a single
fuller length delivery on off; Mhatre goes hard on the slog and gets a thick inside edge to mid-wicket
back of a length and angling well down leg; past Mhatre's pull and has been wided
drilled; tad too full from Arshdeep; it's right in the slot and he bludgeons it straight of mid-off; bounces away to the ropes
the inswinging yorker; Mhatre squeezes it in front of mid-off for a quick single
on a good length and just outside off; steered square on the off-side for a single
yorker length delivery outside off - Mhatre jams the bat down and gets an inside edge onto the on-side
Arshdeep keeps it very full and on off; jammed in front of mid-on and they're off for a quick single
massive; banged in short and Mhatre takes on Jansen - stays on the front-foot; swivels across and then hammers the pull over square leg; went sailing a long way into the stands
a heave and a miss; fuller length delivery and shaping across the right-hander; Mhatre's feet doesn't move as he swung across the line and missed
fraction fuller and just outside off; hit slightly high on Mhatre's bat as he looked to drive. He finds the gap wide of cover though and comes back for the second
on a good length and angling into the right-hander; turned onto the on-side

plenty of height and Mhatre has the distance as well; pitched up anbd angling into the right-hander; was a floaty slower ball; but it's right in the slot and Mhatre heaves it over wide mid-on - drops over the ropes for a maximum
Jansen goes full and just outside off - Mhatre jams the bat down and toe-ends it to the off-side
tossed up on middle; Gaikwad gets down low and sweeps it backward of square on the on-side for a run
tossed up on middle and leg; chipped down to long-on for a single
fraction short and at the stumps; Gaikwad goes back and whips it down to long-on for a single
Chahal slows down the pace around off; punched off the back-foot to deep cover
floaty delivery outside off - driven down to long-off for a single
Chahal slows down the pace on off - Mhatre backs away and cuts it square on the off-side for a single
a maximum and Mhatre gets to a fifty. Stoinis starts with a slower ball; but it's too full and right in the slot; lets Mhatre get forward and then hammer the drive over long-off

back-to-back sixes for Mhatre; he's on a roll now - pitched up by Stoinis and Mhatre got under it; might've sliced it slightly; but he's always looking to go straight and over the bowler's head; sails over the ropes for a maximum
Stoinis shortens the length and pushes one across the right-hander; past the tramlines and has been wided
a low full-toss and Mhatre doesn't get the cross-batted swipe right; came right off the toe-end and plugged in front of long-on
short of a good length and angling into the right-hander - Gaikwad presses across and drops it towards mid-wicket; played with soft hands and he's back for the second
back of a length and well wide of off; pulled down to long-on for a single
banged in short by Stoinis - Mhatre swivels across on the pull and misses; 17 off the over
tossed up on the leg-stump line; Gaikwad makes room and drives it to the right of long-on

a chance and Vyshak has fumbled it; was late to react; runs forward and then dives. He did get to the ball; but it pops out as he hits the ground. Was a lovely leg-break; dropped and turned across the right-hander; Mhatre was shaping for the slog-sweep and only got a top-edge
a loopy delivery wide of off; thrown wide and Mhatre reaches out on the drive; carts it over cover for a couple
slower and at the stumps - Mhatre goes hard on the sweep and drags an inside edge; to the right of short fine
Chahal tosses one on middle and leg; worked into the gap wide of mid-wicket and he's back for the second
slower and wider of off; punched down to long-off for a single
131.6ks back of a length around off; Gaikwad directs it square and gets Mhatre on strike
134.7ks very full delivery; close to yorker length; Mhatre jams it to the off-side and the pair quickly cross over
133ks full pitcher close to off; Gaikwad flicks wide of long-on and picks up a brace
134ks back of a length delivery; Gaikwad punches it to long-on
neatly done! Low full toss outside off; Mhatre reaches for it and opens the bat face; uses the pace on the ball and guides it behind square on the off-side; beats deep backward point comfortably
136.2ks full and outside off; Mhatre walks across and adjusts to play the reverse lap; but does not connect. Vyshak went wider as he saw Mhatre shuffle across and hence no wide call

ball_event: out Caught by Nehal Wadhera!! The partnership is broken. It is that man - Chahal - who does it again. How many times have we seen it in the IPL? Whenever his team needs a wicket
event_info: Chahal steps up. He had bowled well in his first two overs and that pressure got him this wicket. This was not a wicket-taking delivery - drifting down leg; Gaikwad sits down and sweeps it towards long leg. Wadhera runs to his right and takes a good catch while on the move. The idea was to hit that finer but Gaikwad could not get the placement right. Ruturaj Gaikwad c Nehal Wadhera b Yuzvendra Chahal 28(22) [4s-2]
floated up outside off; Dube leans forward and eases it to long-off
uhhh! Nice drift and turn - lands outside leg and turns across; Mhatre stays back and defends it down the pitch

dropped! Tough chance and Shashank can't hold on. Second let off for Mhatre against Chahal. Tosses it up and invites the youngster for the big shot; Mhatre shimmies down and looks to go down the ground; but the ball spins away and Mhatre ends up slicing it over extra cover. Shashank runs in; slides and gets both hands but does not hold on. Was the slide needed? Shashank was charging in and could have got there; but the slide takes him off-balance
fired full around leg; Dube mistimes the sweep to short fine; almost drags it off the inner half and finds the fielder
darted down leg; Dube lets it pass and Prabhsimran does not glove it; the ball deflects away and the pair cross over
1 run; flighted delivery around off; Mhatre eases it to extra cover and Chahal runs behind it; they manage to keep it down to a single. But Chahal has overstepped. Free-hit coming up..
fired down leg; Dube moves inside the line and lets it pass; wide called. Free-hit stays..
low full toss outside off; Dube sits down and toe-ends the sweep to long-on
127.7ks angled in from round the wicket; Dube clips it in front of square
in the air and wide of backward point. Very full delivery outside off; Mhatre reaches and slices the drive behind square. He evades the diving fielder and the ball races away..

ball_event: out Caught by Yuzvendra Chahal!! Vyshak strikes back! Chahal hangs on. A fine knock comes to an end. Both set batters sent back in successive overs. Punjab Kings fighting back. Another very full delivery
event_info: close to yorker length and Mhatre tries to repeat his sliced shot; opens the bat face and picks out short third. The ball was travelling but Chahal does well to take it calmly with his fingers pointing upwards. Ayush Mhatre c Yuzvendra Chahal b Vijaykumar Vyshak 73(43) [4s-6 6s-5]

CSK lose a review. That was needless. Vyshak angles it down leg; Kartik moves inside the line and misses the glance; the ball brushes the trouser on the way to Prabhsimran. There was an enquiry for caught behind from him and Vyshak; but they don't go for it. Kartik does and costs his side a review..
on a length and around off; Kartik guides it to third man and gets off the mark
angled in from round the wicket; Dube tucks it to mid-wicket and retains strike
133.5ks fired full on off; Dube flicks it between deep mid-wicket and long-on; easy brace
Jansen is aiming for the feet; another very full delivery and Dube digs it out to mid-wicket; the ball barely leaves the 30-yard circle and the duo race back for the second
now a slower ball on the pads; Dube clips it to deep square leg

ball_event: out Lbw!! Jansen was very confident. And that is an excellent review. CSK losing their way a bit after that partnership between Mhatre and Gaikwad. This is a good length ball
event_info: which is angled in from round the wicket; Kartik crouches low and misses the swipe across the line; struck right in front of the stumps. The umpire thought the angle was taking it down leg; but he was wrong as the ball-tracker showed it to be smashing into leg-stump. Kartik Sharma lbw b Marco Jansen 1(3)
slower ball slanted in; Sarfaraz gently turns it to mid-wicket
134.3ks good length ball around off; Sarfaraz defends down the pitch
starts with a short of length delivery; Dube swivels around and plays a controlled pull backward of square

shot! He will take his chances. Shimmies down the wicket; gives himself a bit of room and slams it over mid-off. He converted that into a length delivery and gets enough elevation to clear the infield
neatly done! This is classic Sarfaraz. Short ball angled in; he arches back and ramps it over the keeper. He loves playing this shot and connected really well

hat-trick of fours. Now a steer which is played late and the ball runs away. Short of length delivery; gets a bit big on Sarfaraz; but he plays it with soft hands and with an open bat face; splits the keeper and short third perfectly
136ks full toss outside off; Sarfaraz drives in front of square and picks out sweeper
120.1ks short of length delivery
full delivery at the stumps; Dube flicks in front of square and picks up a couple; placed it between deep mid-wicket and long-on
135.2ks full and angled outside off; Dube reaches with laden feet and under-edges it back to Prabhsimran
123.9ks pace off short ball; Dube stands tall and pats it to backward point
132.9ks yorker length around off; Dube digs it out to long-off
back of the hand slower ball; sprayed outside off; Sarfaraz shimmies down and lets it pass; wide given
he's dealing in boundaries. 101.1ks slower ball angled in; Sarfaraz charges down and whips it off his pads; gets it off the middle and the ball sails over the deep backward square fence
134.1ks full toss on the pads; Sarfaraz flicks it straight to deep backward square

cheeky! He loves playing those ramps and steers behind the wicket. Full delivery around off; Sarfaraz is absolutely rooted with his feet; but opens the bat face at the last second and guides it wide of short third
even luck is favouring him. This was a wild hack and the top-edge flies away fine. Sarfaraz was looking to swipe it across the line and the ball flies over Prabhsimran

he's peppering that third man fence. Another boundary in that region.. Any length and the ball is going in that direction. This was back of a length and Sarfaraz still managed to get it fine. He got his hands low and elevated it to the left of short third

ball_event: out Caught by Nehal Wadhera!! Vyshak has the last say. But Sarfaraz has done his job - 12-ball 32 to get the impetus back into the innings. With third man being sent back
event_info: Sarfaraz had to find a different area to play his shots. Looks to go down the ground but the back of a length delivery gets big on him; skies off the upper half and Wadhera takes a few steps back from mid-on to take the catch. Sarfaraz Khan c Nehal Wadhera b Vijaykumar Vyshak 32(12) [4s-6 6s-1]
short ball angled in from round the wicket; Prashant drops his hand and sways away to let it pass
135.4ks short ball slanted across; gets it to climb and Prashant is not able to get on top of the bounce; misses the steer to third man and is beaten on the top-edge
short ball sprayed outside off; Dube lets it pass at the last second and it is called a wide for line
Jansen misses the yorker and is sent sailing into the crowd. 133ks full delivery; right in the slot and Dube clears his front leg; uses the long handle and deposits it into the deep mid-wicket fence
127.2ks short of length angled across; Dube flat-bats it to long-off

Prashant is off the mark in IPL. 134.6ks short of length around off; he moves across his stumps and plays the lap; the ball gets big on him but he managed to glove it fine; beats the fielder inside the ring and the ball races away

Punjab Kings lose a review. Jansen was not interested. But Prabhsimran wanted it checked and he loses his side a review. Full delivery around leg; Prashant sits down and misses the lap. Struck on the pad and the ball rolls down the pitch. The ball had landed outside leg and the impact too was outside leg-stump. Very poor review..
fullish delivery around off; Prashant drills it to long-on
110.1ks slower ball outside off; Dube eases it to deep extra cover and retains strike

shot! 129.8ks full and outside off; right in the slot and Dube did not try to muscle the ball; he saw the vacant area near deep extra cover and decides to just chip it over the infield. The timing is very good as the ball races away..
low full toss outside off; Dube reaches and slices it with an open bat face in front of square; couple more
130.6ks short of length outside off; Dube flat-bats it over the off-side field; just a single
133.8ks short ball angled in; Prashant takes it on and gloves the pull onto the helmet. The ball rolls towards short third and Dube crosses over
too full and too straight from Bartlett; Dube gets inside the line and then clips it backward of square on the on-side; beats the fielder in the ring and runs away to the ropes
sprays the low full toss outside off; Dube lets it pass and collects the wide

very full and past the tramlines as Dube shuffled across; he leaves and it's called as a wide. Kings though contest it and they've reviewed. Would've been a wide had he stayed at his original stance; but since he moved; the wide line has moved as well. Just inside the tramline and the wide call has been revoked. Good call from the Punjab Kings

1 run; banged in short and at the left-hander - Dube swings and misses; they run through for a bye. The keeper has a shy at the striker's end and misses - Dube would've been short with a direct hit; but he'll pinch strike
Punjab Kings lose a review. Full and outside off; Dube did not shuffle much as he lets it pass. The ball is not within the marker and the umpire makes the right call

200 up for CSK. This was not off the middle but Dube packs a punch. Length delivery; right in the slot; Dube heaves it towards deep mid-wicket and the ball trickles into the rope. He has a look at the bottom of his bat
134.5ks very full delivery outside off; Dube swings off his feet and does not make contact
133.9ks yorker outside off; Dube bends down and squeezes it straight to backward point
streaky but they all count. 134.6ks low full toss around off; Dube throws everything behind the big shot; gets a thick inside-edge and the ball escapes past the stumps; races to fine leg
Dube finishes with a boundary. Slower ball; but it is in the slot. Dube delays his bat swing and connects it off the middle; clears mid-on comfortably and the fielder getting across in the deep
short of a good length and angling down leg; past Priyansh's flick and it's called as a wide
beautifully played; the inswinger from Khaleel; but Arya watches it closely; plays with a straight bat and drives it back past the non-striker for a boundary
lovely; short of a good length and angling back into the left-hander - Priyansh swivels across; picks the length and then pummels the pull over mid-wicket for a maximum
short of a good length and angling into the left-hander; tucked wide of mid-wicket for a single
on a good length and just outside off - pushed into the off-side
beaten; Khaleel goes full and gets it to seam away from the right-hander; Prabhsimran's feet went nowhere as he poked and missed
Khaleel sprays one well wide of off; it swings away further and Sanju Samson has to dive to get a hand onto it; prevents extra wides
fuller length delivery and shaping away from wide of off; was close to the tramlines and has been wided
on a good length and swinging away from wide of off; draws Prabhsimran into the drive and goes past the outside edge
edged and four; short of a good length and angling across the left-hander - Priyansh flays from the crease; gets a thick edge and the ball flies in the gap between the keeper and short third
clubbed; short of a good length and just outside off - Priyansh swivels across; picked the length early and then hammered the pull over mid-wicket for a maximum
cracking shot; pitched up by Henry - Priyansh stays leg-sided and then caresses the drive through cover; all timing and the ball runs away to the ropes
short of a good length and at the stumps; pushed in front of mid-on for a single
nicely played; this is a rollicking start from the Kings; tad too short from Henry - Prabhsimran swivels across; uses the pace and then pulls it behind square on the on-side for a boundary
short of a good length and just outside off; steered backward of square on the off-side for a single; 20 off the over
fuller length delivery on the pads; whipped straight to the fielder at mid-wicket. Noor fumbles; but they decide against a single
on a good length and around off; dropped in front of cover and they're off for a quick single
short of a good length and on off; nudged in front of mid-wicket for a single
a swing and a miss; short of a good length and pushed across the right-hander - Prabhsimran swings hard and misses
tad shorter and just outside off - chopped behind square on the off-side for a single

he's dealing in sixes at the moment; Khaleel drags the length back and seemed to have cramped Priyansh for room on the pull; but it's fast hands on display as the left-hander swivels onto the shot; high and over the ropes at mid-wicket for a maximum
drilled; but Kamboj will feel he should've stopped it; was full and just outside off and Prabhsimran hammered it back at the bowler. Burst through his hands on the followthrough
smashed; they're toying with the bowlers at the moment; on a good length and just outside off - Prabhsimran shuffles across; stands steady and smashes it back over the bowler's head for a boundary
back of a length and just outside off - steered off the back-foot and straight to the fielder at backward point
the boundaries are flowing at the moment; fuller length delivery and well wide of off - no one in the deep and Prabhsimran slices the drive over point
fuller length delivery outside off; driven uppishly and to the left of the bowler. Kamboj stuck his hand out; but that'd have been a ripper had he held on
short of a good length and just outside off - Prabhsimran looks to run it down to the off-side and is undone by the extra bounce

Henry continues to travel. Change of ends for him; but he's welcomed with another maximum by Priyansh - length ball on off and it's lifted back over the bowler's head; sails over the straight boundary for a maximum

ball_event: out Bowled!! Rush of blood and Priyansh has thrown his wicket away. He probably got carried away and a promising knock has been cut short. On a length and angling across the left-hander - Priyansh charged down the pitch
event_info: made room and missed the ball completely; the off-stump is knocked out and Henry celebrates with gusto. Priyansh Arya b Matt Henry 39(11) [4s-3 6s-4]
1 run; short of a good length and angling into the left-hander; Connolly was looking to flick and missed; the ball rolls to the off-side
fuller length delivery outside off; Prabhsimran heaves across the line and misses; bounces through to the keeper

a celebappeal for LBW - Henry was convinced; but the umpire turns it down. He convinces Ruturaj into a review. Was it going down leg? Don't think there's bat and UltraEdge confirms the same. The impact is in front of leg-stump but Prabhsimran's back-leg was pretty deep; ball-tracking has it flicking the leg-stump; stays with the on-field call and he survives. CSK don't lose a review though
fuller length delivery outside off; driven on the bounce to mid-of where Dube fumbles and allows a single
short of a good length and angling across the right-hander - Prabhsimran comes down the pitch; has a swish and misses
thumped; the slower ball from Khaleel; but it's too full and wide of off - Prabhsimran drives and clears cover; one bounce and over the ropes
the slower cutter now; pushed across the right-hander and doesn't bounce a lot - Prabhsimran swipes across the line and misses

appeal for caught behind; Khaleel was convinced; but Sanju Samson wasn't and they decide against a review. Was a length delivery that was pushed across the right-hander - Prabhsimran has a hack and misses
on a good length and just outside off - Prabhsimran once again goes hard on the drive and mistimes it to cover
short of a good length and just outside off - Connolly goes hard on the pull and mistimes it to mid-off
tossed up on middle; Prabhsimran plays with soft hands and nudges it past square leg for a single
flighted delivery on middle; worked off the back-foot past mid-wicket
clubbed; pushed through by Noor - drifts across the right-hander and Prabhsimran latches onto the pull; hits it high and wide of mid-wicket for a maximum
tossed up and sliding across the right-hander - Prabhsimran went across on the slog-sweep and missed; struck the fore-arm and then ricocheted onto the helmet
tossed up well outside off - Prabhsimran leans across and pushes it to cover

nicely played; Prabhsimran goes for the reverse sweep; very full and just outside off - almost went through Prabhsimran's defences; but he gets the bat down in time; rides the bounce and pierces the gap behind point for a boundary
dragged short and just outside off; pulled down to long-on for a single

appeal for LBW; but the impact seemed to be outside the line of off-stump - tossed up just outside off and Prabhsimran was shaping for the big reverse sweep. Missed and was struck just outside the line of off-stump
flighted delivery on off; punched down to long-on for a single
pushed through by Chahar - Connolly goes back and nudges it through mid-wicket for a single
the leg-break and Prabhsimran was nearly undone by the grip - slower and wider of off; Prabhsimran went hard on the drive and carved it over point; on the bounce to the fielder in the deep
cracking shot; dragged short by Chahar - Connolly rocks back and then clatters the cut over cover; bounces away to the ropes in a hurry
fraction short and wide of off; Prabhsimran waits on it and then cuts it late; beats cover-point to his left and Kamboj does well in the deep to slide and cut off the boundary
tossed up well wide of off; Connolly went hard on the drive and the inside edge bounces past the keeper
Noor tosses one just outside off; worked into the gap wide of mid-wicket for a single
Noor drags the length back on off; pulled down to long-on for a single

ball_event: out Prabhsimran Singh Run Out!! 1 run completed. Oh dear! A terrible mix-up and Prabhsimran has been run-out. Was his call for the second
event_info: but his partner wasn't interested one bit. Sends him back; but it's too late - Sarfaraz with the throw from mid-wicket and Ruturaj breaks the stumps; finding Prabhsimran short by at least a couple of yards. Was played with soft hands to mid-wicket and a slight fumble by a chasing Noor prompted Prabhsimran to look for a second. Prabhsimran Singh run out (Sarfaraz Khan/Ruturaj Gaikwad) 43(34) [4s-6 6s-1]
flighted delivery outside off; Connolly picks out cover with the drive
tossed up well wide of off; driven square on the off-side for a single
pushed through quicker and just outside off - Connolly was shaping for the cut and missed
Chahar tosses one well outside off - swiped away through mid-wicket for a single
the wrong 'un; slides onto the pads and it's clipped behind square on the on-side
hammered; tad too full from Chahar; right in the slot and Connolly drills it back over the bowler's head for a boundary
the wrong 'un; spins away and catches Connolly's edge as he looked to work it onto the on-side; a single wide of cover

Connolly getting a move on. Boundaries in successive overs for him. This was fraction short; the line is outside off and Connolly unleashes a fierce cut; places it backward of point and the ball races away
93.7ks googly around off; Connolly mistimes the lofted drive to long-on; goes on the bounce
96.3ks leg-lined delivery; Iyer clips it to deep mid-wicket

FOUR; almost a repeat of the previous boundary. Marginally short and Connolly has the space to play the cut; hits it behind square and once again Khaleel is beaten to the fence. Oh no! Noor has overstepped. So; a free-hit is coming up..
96.8ks shortens the length; Connolly flat-bats it straight down the ground but not off the middle; the ball lands short of the long-off fielder and the duo pick up a couple
wrong 'un which is short of length; Connolly mistimes the pull to deep mid-wicket
94.8ks flighted delivery on off; Iyer prods forward and defends to the leg-side
that's a gift - short and wide outside off; Connolly accepts it happily. Third man was inside the ring and so was backward point; he goes up and over with the cut shot; one bounce and over the ropes
back of a length angled across; Connolly flat-bats it to long-off
fraction short and around hip-height; Iyer plays a one-legged pull to deep square leg
Connolly outsmarts Kamboj. Square leg was brought in; so Connolly shuffles across and laps it backward of square - splitting backward square leg and short fine perfectly

ball_event: out Caught by Matt Henry!! Oh no! That's not a wicket-taking delivery. But CSK won't be complaining as a well-set Connolly is walking back to the dugout. A juicy full toss around off
event_info: Connolly looks to loft it down the ground and gets it high on the bat; the ball flies to long-off where Henry comes in and takes a simple catch. The top three all got starts but none have converted it into a big score. Cooper Connolly c Matt Henry b Anshul Kamboj 36(22) [4s-6]
short of length close to off; Wadhera rides the bounce and guides it to backward point
90.5ks tossed up on off; Iyer eases it towards the non-striker. Sarfaraz gets around from mid-wicket and makes the stop
91.6ks fuller and around off; Iyer lofts it back over Wadhera and Mhatre tidies up in the deep; easy couple

lovely shot! Chahar has dismissed Iyer twice in 5 innings. But Iyer seems to be more cautious today. He sees the floaty delivery and lofts it with the spin over extra cover. Packs enough power for the ball to sail into the stands
flighted close to off; Iyer opens the bat face and guides it towards short third; Henry manages to make the stop
tossed up on off; Wadhera eases the drive down to long-off
second six in the over. Iyer is winning today's battle against Chahar. This is dragged short and Iyer is quick to pounce - rocks back and hammers the pull over deep square leg

they run of a misfield and get away with it. Floated up outside off; Wadhera slices the drive to backward point and Dube fumbles. Iyer calls for the risky single and the throw goes to the non-striker's end. A direct hit and Wadhera was a goner
short and drifting away; bit of extra bounce; Iyer attempts the cut and is beaten on the top-edge
sprays the googly outside off; Iyer lets it pass and pockets the wide
flighted around leg; Iyer nudges it with soft hands to deep square leg
googly close to off; Wadhera chops it to backward point and Iyer quickly crosses over
wrong 'un outside off; Iyer opens the bat face and eases it to sweeper cover
short of length googly; Wadhera has no room and knocks it to the left of Noor
short of length delivery; the line is outside off and Iyer has the space to play the pull; places it wide of long-on and collects a brace
full pitcher outside off; Iyer drives wide of long-off and Dube fumbles; not the first time today; concedes the extra run
very full delivery around off; Iyer squeezes the drive to extra cover; straight to Gaikwad
nicely done! Slower ball bowled into the pitch; Iyer waits and ramps it over short third. There was no pace but Iyer just had to clear the inner ring which he managed to do with ease
smashed! Short of length outside off; sits up nicely for Iyer to play the pull; he had the width to have a free swing of his hands; nails the pull flat and wide of deep mid-wicket
full toss at the stumps; Iyer skews it off the outer half to sweeper cover

Iyer has turned it on in the last few overs. He's taken over from Connolly. Short and outside off; Iyer cuts it square on the off-side and Sarfaraz can't make the stop. He gets around from sweeper but can't prevent the boundary; allows it to escape under him
googly on off; Iyer waits and works it with the spin to long-on

Wadhera tees off and sends it sailing over the rope. Punjab Kings are on course for their second win of the season. Floated up; lands right in the slot and Wadhera brings out the long handle; lofts down the ground and leaves the long-on fielder ball-watching
eased to long-on
fired outside off; Iyer ignores and picks up the wide
fuller and outside off; Iyer drives to long-off and brings up the 50-run stand
googly outside off; turns away and Wadhera misses the big swipe across the line

excellent placement! Kamboj comes round the wicket; angles the full delivery across; but Iyer is up for it; he walks to the off-side and whips off his pads towards cow corner; beats the fielder in the deep and collects the boundary
Punjab Kings lose a review. Kamboj nails the wide yorker; Iyer moves across and fails to dig it out. He thought it was a away from the marker and decided to challenge the onfield call

dropped! That needs to be taken. Henry is quite fine at short third - almost at fly slip position. Kamboj goes for the wide yorker; Iyer bends down and slices it with an open bat face. The ball just carries to Henry and he does not hang on. Just gets his fingertips and the catch goes down

fifty for Iyer. He missed out in the first game but not in the first away game. He's taking his team over the line. Very similar delivery to the previous one - yorker outside off; but this time Iyer decides to go aerial; he goes up and over with the slice; clears Henry at short third comfortably and he reaches the milestone

yorker around leg-stump; Iyer moves inside the line and misses the glance. Excellent take by Sanju Samson behind the stumps - he dives to his left and gloves it with one hand. But Kamboj has overstepped. Free-hit coming up..
shortens the length and angles it across; Iyer takes it on and has a slash; but the extra bounce takes it over the top-edge and the ball thuds into Samson's mitts

ball_event: out Caught by Rahul Chahar!! Kamboj strikes back! But it might be too late for Chennai Super Kings. The wide line finally pays off. Kamboj angles it across from round the wicket
event_info: the length is full but not quite in the yorker zone; Iyer reaches for it and mistimes the loft. These are big square boundaries at Chepauk and Iyer does not get enough to clear sweeper cover; Chahar settles under it and takes it safely. A fine knock from the captain comes to an end. Shreyas Iyer c Rahul Chahar b Anshul Kamboj 50(29) [4s-4 6s-3]

ball_event: out Caught by Noor Ahmad!! Hang on! Is there still life left in this contest? Chennai Super Kings and their fans certainly believe. The Chepauk has found its voice again. Fuller ball slanted across
event_info: Wadhera shimmies down and drives it uppishly; straight to extra cover and Noor makes no mistake. Back to back wickets for CSK. Nehal Wadhera c Noor Ahmad b Matt Henry 10(9) [6s-1]

not where he intended but Stoinis won't mind it. Full and angled across; Stoinis throws the kitchen sink on the drive and gets a thick outside-edge; the ball flies wide of short third and races to the fence
full toss around off; Stoinis mistimes the drive to the left of Henry and he fumbles; single conceded
in the air and safe. Full and slanted outside off; Shashank goes up and over extra cover. Not quite off the middle and hence the ball drops in no man's land; couple more

another edge; another boundary. The plan is to bowl wide and make the batters hit to the longer side; but at the moment the outside-edge is flying to the rope. Shashank throws everything behind the big shot and the edge flies over short third
low full toss outside off; Shashank drives to sweeper cover and retains strike

low full toss outside off; Shashank has to reach and he does so; opens the bat face and slices the drive square on the off-side. The two fielders in the deep are at deep backward point and deep extra cover
full and at the stumps; Shashank flicks it in front of square and picks up couple more

short of length down leg; Shashank attempts to help it along and does not connect. CSK have reviewed. It is for a wide call. Samson suggested they have two reviews; so why not! Gaikwad signals the T. Shashank did not move nor did the ball hit anything on the way to Samson. The onfield decision stays.. The review is lost
length delivery around leg; Shashank swings hard and drags it off the inner half to deep backward square

Punjab Kings win by 5 wickets. Stoinis seals the victory with the a thumping shot. Kamboj goes over the wicket; lands it right in the slot and Stoinis has no half measures; slams it straight back over the bowler and makes it two wins out of two for his team
